name: Bitfinex Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Cryptocurrency exchange and trading platform REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and institutions to access spot, margin trading (up to 10x leverage), derivatives, and peer-to-peer funding markets, with comprehensive REST API v2 for order management, WebSocket v2 for real-time streaming, and unique P2P lending market where users can lend margin funding to other traders.
use_cases: Executing margin trades via Bitfinex API with leverage up to 10x for sophisticated strategies
Providing P2P margin funding via Bitfinex Funding API for yield on crypto holdings
Accessing professional-grade derivatives via Bitfinex Derivatives API for institutional trading
Streaming real-time market data via Bitfinex WebSocket v2 for high-frequency trading
Managing complex order types via Bitfinex API (iceberg, TWAP, trailing stop) for algo trading
not_for: Beginners or retail traders without derivatives/margin experience
US users (Bitfinex withdrew from US market in 2017 — use Coinbase or Kraken)
Simple fiat-to-crypto purchases (Bitfinex is primarily professional trading focused)
best_when: A professional or institutional trader needs a mature exchange with margin trading, P2P funding markets, and advanced order types — particularly outside the US market.
avoid_when: You're in the US, need simple retail-friendly interface, or require strict regulatory compliance with transparent jurisdiction.

auth.notes: API Key + API Secret for HMAC-SHA384 signing. bfx-nonce (Unix millisecond timestamp). bfx-apikey header. bfx-signature: HMAC-SHA384 of '/api/' + path + nonce + body. v2 signature differs from v1 (incompatible). REST v2 base: api.bitfinex.com/v2/. WebSocket v2: wss://api-pub.bitfinex.com/ws/2. API key permissions: Read, Write, Withdraw. No OAuth2. No webhooks.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; maker fee 0.10% / taker 0.20% (volume tiers reduce to 0%/0.055%)
pricing.paid_tiers: Volume-based fee tiers; no subscription needed
pricing.notes: British Virgin Islands incorporated. Founded 2012. Historically one of the largest BTC exchanges by volume. Controlled by iFinex Inc. Associated with Tether (USDT) stablecoin controversy — same parent company. 2016 hack: $72M in BTC stolen (UNUS SED LEO token issued to compensate). Continued operating. Withdrew from US market 2017. P2P funding market unique feature — lend margin funding to other traders. Advanced order types: limit, market, stop, trailing stop, fill-or-kill, iceberg, TWAP. Derivatives: perpetuals, futures. Professional trading focus. API v2 is the current standard (v1 deprecated). WebSocket v2 with channel subscriptions.

performance.rate_limits: 10-90 requests per minute depending on endpoint; public endpoints more lenient

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA384 signing. v2 API (incompatible with v1). bfx-nonce/bfx-apikey/bfx-signature headers. US market withdrawn 2017. 2016 hack history. BVI jurisdiction. Tether association controversy. 10-90 req/min limit.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation not idempotent — retries create duplicates. client_order_id (cid) for deduplication attempt. Cancel idempotent by order ID.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC-SHA384 (not common SHA256/SHA512): signature = HMAC-SHA384('/api/' + path + nonce + body)
v2 signature format is INCOMPATIBLE with v1 — do not mix versions
bfx-nonce must be monotonically increasing — use millisecond timestamp
US users blocked — Bitfinex withdrew from US market in 2017
Rate limits vary widely by endpoint (10/min for private, 90/min for some public) — check docs
2016 $72M hack: company issued UNUS SED LEO token to compensate users — reputational history

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA384 signing. bfx-nonce prevents replay attacks. BVI jurisdiction (limited regulatory oversight). US market withdrawal. 2016 $72M hack (rebuilt security after). Tether/USDT controversy (same parent company — potential systemic risk). API permissions (read/write/withdraw). Cryptocurrency financial data — high value target.
